stories.headline: Charles Schwab Opens Spot BTC and ETH Trading to Retail Clients
stories.tldr: Schwab rolled out direct spot Bitcoin and Ether trading for retail accounts, with custody in-house and execution routed to Paxos.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Schwab's 35M-plus brokerage base now gets native crypto alongside equities, collapsing the onboarding gap between TradFi and on-chain assets.
the_details: Launch covers spot BTC and ETH, integrated inside existing Schwab brokerage portfolios.
Schwab self-custodies client crypto; Paxos handles execution on the trading venue side.
Move lands two years after Schwab filed for its spot Bitcoin ETF and mirrors Fidelity's retail flow.

stories.headline: SEC Carves Broker-Dealer Exception for Decentralized Trading Interfaces
stories.tldr: The SEC said it will not object to 'Covered User Interface Providers' operating frontends for crypto asset securities without broker-dealer registration.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: First explicit US carve-out separating protocol frontends from broker-dealer obligations, de-risking the Uniswap-style UI stack.
the_details: Statement dated April 13, 2026 follows the joint SEC-CFTC interpretive framework on digital asset taxonomy.
Applies to software interfaces that only let users prepare and submit transactions in crypto asset securities.
Operationalizes the Howey test so assets can transition in and out of securities status over time.

stories.headline: Rhea Finance Loses $7.6M in Fake-Contract Exploit; Tether Freezes $3.29M USDT
stories.tldr: Attackers drained at least $7.6M from Rhea Finance using spoofed contracts and seeded liquidity before Tether blacklisted $3.29M USDT tied to the thieves.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Same fake-asset playbook used against Drift's $285M hack earlier this month — oracle and listing hygiene is now the top DeFi risk vector.
the_details: Exploit used fake token contracts and thin liquidity to trick Rhea's pricing into over-collateralizing loans.
Tether blacklisted ~3.29M USDT linked to exploiter wallets within hours of on-chain detection.
Second major fake-token oracle exploit in April, after Drift Protocol's $285M loss on April 1.

stories.headline: Fake Ledger Live App on Apple App Store Drained $9.5M From 50+ Wallets
stories.tldr: A counterfeit Ledger Live app that passed Apple's review cleared at least $9.5M across BTC, ETH, SOL, TRX and XRP wallets between April 7-13.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Apple's vetting was supposed to be the security floor — the breach shreds the 'App Store is safe by default' assumption for self-custody tools.
the_details: Victims entered seed phrases into the malicious app, giving attackers full signing access across 5+ chains.
CoinDesk and Ledger flagged the listing on April 14 after loss patterns became visible on-chain.
At least 50 confirmed victims; total stolen may climb as more wallets report unauthorized sweeps.

stories.headline: Axelar Rips 29%, BIO Protocol 17% as Fear Index Hits 23 'Extreme Fear'
stories.tldr: Axelar (AXL) surged 29.14% and BIO Protocol 17.52% over 24 hours even as the Crypto Fear & Greed Index dropped to 23 and BTC dominance held 58.5%.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Targeted rotations into interop and DeSci names are breaking out despite 'Bitcoin Season' on CMC's index — pockets of alpha exist while macro sentiment stays bearish.
the_details: Total crypto market cap sits at $2.63T, up 0.6% on $120.3B of 24h volume.
CMC Altcoin Season Index reads 34/100 — still Bitcoin Season — while BTC trades near $74,768.
BIO is the flagship DeSci asset, pitching on-chain scientific IP and research funding rails.
